signals.raw:
South Korea Announces 25 Trillion Won Supplementary Budget | YES | MED confidence**  
Minister Park Hong-geun confirmed a 25 trillion won ($16.6 billion) stimulus package on March 27, 2026, to counter inflation and growth risks without raising public debt, amid OECD's lowered GDP forecast to 1.7% from 2.1%.[1] Effectiveness is doubted by economists due to past stimulus failures during energy shock

HEADLINE:** Solana Hits All-Time Record of 167 Million Monthly Token Holders Amid Alpenglow Upgrade Launch | YES | HIGH confidence

Solana has reached **167 million monthly token holders** with daily non-vote transactions hitting historic highs of **148 million transactions**[1]. The highly anticipated **Alpenglow consensus upgrade** has been deployed, aiming to reduce transaction finality from 12

signals.headline: HEADLINE:** Bitcoin Breaks Above $75,000; Ethereum Surges 8
signals.raw:
HEADLINE:** Bitcoin Breaks Above $75,000; Ethereum Surges 8.6% to $2,377; Solana Rises 6.3% | YES | HIGH confidence

The broader crypto market rally was triggered by Iran signaling willingness to resume peace talks with the United States[2]. **Ethereum outperformed all major tokens** with an 8.6% jump, while **Solana climbed 6.3%**[2]. Bitcoin achieved its highest level since mid-March[2].

signals.headline: HEADLINE:** Solana Overtakes Ethereum in Stablecoin Volume; Total Supply Hits Record $16
signals.raw:
HEADLINE:** Solana Overtakes Ethereum in Stablecoin Volume; Total Supply Hits Record $16.288 Billion | YES | HIGH confidence

Solana captured **32.6% of weekly adjusted USD-based stablecoin volume** (ending April 12), surpassing Ethereum's 27.8%[3]. Total stablecoin supply on Solana reached a record **$16.288 billion**, growing **10.87% in one week**—the fastest growth rate among top-10 chains[3].

signals.headline: South Korea March 2026 Inflation Rises to 2
signals.raw:
South Korea March 2026 Inflation Rises to 2.2%, Below Forecasts | NO | HIGH confidence**  
South Korea's annual inflation increased to 2.2% in March 2026 from 2.0% prior, but fell short of market expectations at 2.4%, per Statistics Korea data.[2] Monthly prices rose 0.3%, with pressures contained near the Bank of Korea's 2% target despite Middle East energy risks.[2]
matched_markets: Will South Korea’s 2026 Annual Inflation be between 2.7% and 2.9%?

signals.headline: OECD Lowers South Korea 2026 GDP to 1
signals.raw:
OECD Lowers South Korea 2026 GDP to 1.7% Amid Inflation Surge | YES | HIGH confidence**  
OECD's interim outlook on March 30, 2026, cut South Korea's GDP growth to 1.7% and raised G20 inflation to 4%, with Korea at 2.7%, citing Middle East conflict and energy import reliance.[1] This aligns with private forecasts like Natixis at 1% and Capital Economics at 1.6%.[1][5]
